data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260513T172704-447ea3a4/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260513T172704-447ea3a4/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260513T172704-447ea3a4/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260513T172704-447ea3a4/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260513T172704-447ea3a4/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 3. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-f07cb5d3">
    <title>Add Story 16 cross-cutting run-detail e2e and p95 fixture coverage</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Story 16 needs an end-to-end guard that the run-detail surface can move from the Runs list into prompt, response, tool trace, and evidence data, and a performance guard for a p95-shaped run with 200 tool calls and 30 evidence files.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/runs.spec.ts:249-473 contains the existing full-stack run-detail browser flow.
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/runs-evidence.spec.ts:207-340 covers evidence whitelist, large-file, and non-whitelisted download-only behavior.
- cli/internal/server/state_runs_detail_test.go:169-227 covers persisted tool-call and drain-time shape at the Go layer.
- The parent bead combines browser e2e, large fixture shape, and global gates; splitting this child keeps it test-only and avoids production frontend work owned by Story 16.1 and Story 16.2.
- Parent context: ddx-b4c5151a, originally split from ddx-70a432b5. Preserve dependency IDs ddx-f04faa86 and ddx-4cd64068 when filing this child.

PROPOSED FIX
Add or update Playwright coverage so a mocked project exercises Runs list -> run detail -> prompt tab -> response tab -> tools tab -> evidence tab with prompt, response, tool trace, and evidence-list data present. Add a p95 fixture with 200 tool calls and 30 evidence files and assert each tab is visible and interactive within one requestAnimationFrame after its mocked data resolves. Keep this child to tests, fixtures, selectors, and harmless test hooks only.

NON-SCOPE
- Production frontend implementation; Story 16.1 and Story 16.2 own the page and evidence tab behavior.
- New auth or role-based access control beyond what the trust boundary documents.
- Backend resolver/audit implementation and FEAT prose updates assigned to sibling child beads.
- Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Add or update a Playwright test named Story16RunDetailFullStackP95 that covers prompt, response, tool-trace, and evidence-list traversal from the Runs list to /runs/[runId].
2. Story16RunDetailFullStackP95 uses a p95 fixture with exactly 200 tool calls and 30 evidence files, and asserts all run-detail tabs render and become interactive within one requestAnimationFrame after data resolution.
3. Add or update TestStory16RunDetailP95FixtureShape to pin the Go-side fixture/resolver shape for 200 tool calls and 30 bundle files.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'TestStory16RunDetailP95FixtureShape|TestRunToolCalls_PersistedAtDrain|TestRunBundleFiles_Listing' passes.
5. cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test:e2e -- runs.spec.ts passes, or cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test:e2e passes if the local script does not support a file argument.
6. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, story:16, area:frontend, area:e2e, area:server, kind:test, spec:FEAT-008, spec:FEAT-010, decomposed, needs-human-decomposition</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-f129bfaf" base-rev="5c17ed2835483c9deab1e9badf33fb462d231411" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260513T172704-447ea3a4"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Commit succeeded. Summary:

- Added Playwright test `Story16RunDetailFullStackP95` in `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/runs.spec.ts` that traverses Runs list → `/runs/[runId]` with a p95 fixture (200 tool calls + 30 evidence files) and asserts each tab renders + becomes interactive within one `requestAnimationFrame` after data resolution.
- Added Go test `TestStory16RunDetailP95FixtureShape` in `cli/internal/server/state_runs_detail_test.go` pinning the resolver shape: 200 ordered tool calls + 30 path-sorted bundle files with mimeType.
- Fixed pre-existing mock-data inconsistency in `tab state survives navigation` so `runs.spec.ts` is fully green.

All ACs verified: target Go tests pass, full Go suite passes, all 8 `runs.spec.ts` tests pass, lefthook pre-commit passes.
